name: **Description
This API endpoint is used to checkout a document from a file cabinet and save it to the file system.
**Integration
checkOutToFileSystem (Basic - Download to File)
- Downloads a document from DocuWare and writes it to disk as a File.
- Calls the REST endpoint /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId}/CheckOutToFileSystem.
- Returns a local File containing the downloaded content.
Parameters:
- configKey (String): REST client configuration key to identify the DocuWare server connection from rest-clients.yaml
- fileCabinetId (String): The unique identifier of the file cabinet where the document resides
- documentId (String): The unique identifier of the document where the stamp will be applied
- Default / Return: Returns a File written to disk; use the stream overload to avoid creating temp files.
**Integration
checkOutToFileSystemAsStream (Basic - Stream Download)
- Downloads a document from DocuWare and returns it as an InputStream plus fileName (no temp file).
- Calls the REST endpoint /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId}/CheckOutToFileSystem.
- Useful for in-memory processing or direct streaming to another service.
Parameters:
- configKey (String): REST client configuration key to identify the DocuWare server connection from rest-clients.yaml
- fileCabinetId (String): The unique identifier of the file cabinet where the document resides
- documentId (String): The unique identifier of the document where the stamp will be applied
- Default / Return: Returns InputStream and fileName (no File created).
**Integration
checkInFromFileSystem (File overload - Multipart Upload)
- Performs a multipart POST to /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId}/CheckInFromFileSystem.
- Multipart includes serialized CheckIn JSON (DocuWareCheckInActionParameters) and the File[] content.
- Uses DocuWareService.checkInFromFileSystem(...) internally to perform the upload and parse the result.
Parameters:
- configKey (String): REST client configuration key to identify the DocuWare server connection from rest-clients.yaml
- fileCabinetId (String): The unique identifier of the file cabinet where the document resides
- documentId (String): The unique identifier of the document where the stamp will be applied
- checkInParameters (DocuWareCheckInActionParameters): Check-in metadata (document version, CheckInReturnDocument enum, comments)
- file (File): Local file to upload
- Return: com.docuware.dev.schema._public.services.platform.Document — DocuWare Document object with updated metadata on success.
**Integration
checkInFromFileSystem (Stream overload - Multipart Upload)
- Performs a multipart POST to /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId}/CheckInFromFileSystem.
- Multipart includes serialized CheckIn JSON (DocuWareCheckInActionParameters) and accepts an InputStream and fileName.
- Uses DocuWareService.checkInFromFileSystem(...) internally to perform the upload and parse the result.
- Preferred when you already hold the file in memory or want to avoid disk IO.
Parameters:
- configKey (String): REST client configuration key to identify the DocuWare server connection from rest-clients.yaml
- fileCabinetId (String): The unique identifier of the file cabinet where the document resides
- documentId (String): The unique identifier of the document where the stamp will be applied
- checkInParameters (DocuWareCheckInActionParameters): Check-in metadata (document version, CheckInReturnDocument enum, comments)
- fileName (String): Name to use for the uploaded file
- stream (InputStream): File content stream
- Return: com.docuware.dev.schema._public.services.platform.Document — updated document metadata.
**How to integrate
Add Call: Add a Call element to your process that invokes CheckinService. Use one of the exposed signatures:
checkOutToFileSystem(String, String, String) — download file to workspace File
checkOutToFileSystemAsStream(String, String, String) — download file as InputStream + filename
checkInFromFileSystem(String, String, DocuWareCheckInActionParameters, String, File) — check in from a local File
checkInFromFileSystem(String, String, String, String, InputStream, DocuWareCheckInActionParameters) — check in from a stream
Prepare inputs:
- configKey (String): REST client config key from config/rest-clients.yaml (the DocuWare UUID).
- fileCabinetId (String): target file cabinet ID.
- documentId (String): target document ID.
- file (File) or stream + fileName: file contents to check in (depending on overload).
- checkInParameters (DocuWareCheckInActionParameters): contains:
+ DocumentVersion / major & minor — version to use for the new check-in
+ CheckInReturnDocument — e.g., CHECKED_IN
+ Comments — optional check-in comment
What the process does:

- For checkout: sends POST to /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId}/CheckOutToFileSystem. If checkOutAsStream is true the result is returned as InputStream + fileName; otherwise a File is created and populated.
- For checkin: builds a multipart request with:
+ CheckIn JSON (serialized DocuWareCheckInActionParameters)
+ File[] content (the file stream or file)
- The actual implementation lives in DocuWareService:
+ checkInFromFileSystem(WebTarget target, DocuWareCheckInActionParameters params, String fileName, InputStream file)
Return values:
+ Checkout (basic): returns a File (downloaded file).
+ Checkout (stream): returns InputStream + fileName.
+ Checkin: returns com.docuware.dev.schema._public.services.platform.Document on success.
After success:
+ The document in DocuWare is checked in with the provided version and comments.
+ Inspect the returned Document to confirm metadata (version, id, links).
Example (conceptual):
- Set variables:
+ configKey = "02d1eec1-32e9-4316-afc3-793448486203"
+ fileCabinetId = "FC-123"
+ documentId = "DOC-456"
+ checkInParameters = DocuWareService.get().createCheckInActionParameters(CheckInReturnDocument.CHECKED_IN, "Checked in by process", 1, 0)
+ file = new File("signed.pdf", true) or read stream into InputStream stream
- Call CheckinService:checkInFromFileSystem(...) with the matching signature.
- After return, read result.document for the updated Document object.
Notes & tips:
- Use checkOutToFileSystemAsStream when you want to avoid creating temporary files.
- Use DocuWareService.createCheckInActionParameters(...) helper to construct proper DocuWareCheckInActionParameters.
- Ensure config/rest-clients.yaml DocuWare entry is configured (URL, auth features).
Summary: documented how to call CheckinService (signatures, inputs, behavior, returns) and provided a concise example and tips.
Task completed: wrote integration documentation for CheckinService.
**Check out
Call REST Client APIs from Docuware.
**Check in
Call REST Client APIs from Docuware.
